hero.title: Your NIP-46 bunker, always on.
hero.body: Sign Nostr events remotely. Your nsec stays encrypted in the vault; the server never stores it in plaintext — only an encrypted blob, with an optional in-memory session (configurable TTL).
hero.infraLead: Prefer to run your own stack?
hero.infraLink: Docker and MIT-licensed open source repo

how.title: How it works
how.subtitle: Three steps — from keypair to remote signing in the BitMacro ecosystem.
how.step1Body: In the browser, the vault creates the keypair and prepares the Nostr Connect URI (NIP-46).
how.step2Body: Display the QR or bunker URI to your Nostr client (phone or desktop).
how.step3Body: Connect via the BitMacro relay for stable signing requests, 24/7, without exposing your nsec.

compare.title: Comparison
compare.intro: BitMacro Signer compared to familiar options in the Nostr ecosystem — features by category.
compare.footnotePhase2: = on the roadmap, not in the MVP.
compare.footnoteDisclaimer: Amber and Alby are independent projects — this compares typical product capabilities, not an absolute ranking.

features.nsecNeverExposed: nsec never exposed to the app

details.unifiedOnboardingDetail: (keypair + NIP-05 + relay + bunker in one flow)

selfHost.title: Run on your infrastructure
selfHost.body: Docker image with Next.js in standalone mode. Copy environment variables, build, and run — no extra steps beyond .env.
selfHost.bullet1: Secrets stay on the host — never commit keys.
selfHost.githubLink: Dockerfile, Compose, and README on GitHub

errors.npubNotRegistered: Npub not registered for this Identity
errors.generateOrReload: Generate the keypair again from the bottom link on the identity section.
errors.missingIdentityStep1: Missing identity_id — unlock again with your npub.
errors.nsecMismatch: This nsec does not match your npub

header.title: Activate the bunker
header.subtitle: Use your BitMacro Identity npub and vault passphrase to get the NIP-46 QR code.
header.sessionManaged: Session active — bunker in managed (server) mode
header.serverNote: The bunker runs on the server — it does not depend on this tab staying open.

step1.title: Identity
step1.freshNpubLink: Don’t have an npub yet? Generate a keypair in the browser
step1.backToUnlock: ← Back to unlock with existing npub
step1.warnSaveNpub: Save this npub — it is your Nostr identity for the bunker. After the vault is set up, you can link it to BitMacro Identity if you want.

step2.title: Import nsec (first vault)
step2.body: There is no encrypted vault for this npub yet. Paste the nsec that matches your npub and choose a passphrase to encrypt the vault.

sessionsPreview.title: Client sessions
sessionsPreview.empty: No sessions yet — generate a bunker link below for your first app.

step3.title: Connect an app
step3.explain: NIP-46 uses a temporary client key (not your profile npub). Generate the QR and paste it in the app — on first connect the client sends that key and the session is bound automatically.
step3.labelHint: The protocol does not send the app name (Primal, Coracle, Nostrudel, …). This label is shown in sessions so you can tell them apart.
step3.qrHelp1: Paste this QR in the client or copy the full link. Each link is single-use: after an app connects successfully, it stops working.
step3.qrHelp2: Another device or app needs a new link. Remove stale bunker connections in the client if it cached an old URI.
step3.newLinkInline: Need a new link for another app?

backup.intro: Your vault is encrypted with your passphrase. BitMacro Signer never stores your nsec in plaintext. This PDF is the portable copy you need to recover your identity without BitMacro infrastructure.
backup.bulletIndependent: The PDF contains your npub (readable) and the same encrypted vault blob as our servers — not your nsec in clear text.
backup.bulletPassphrase: You must keep your vault passphrase: it decrypts the backup. Without it, the PDF is useless.
backup.bulletStorage: Store the PDF offline (USB, printed paper in a safe). Avoid cloud folders if you want stronger security.
backup.bulletNoCloud: Anyone with this file and your passphrase controls your Nostr identity. Do not share.
backup.codeHint: After downloading, type this code to prove you have the file. You never need to paste your nsec.
backup.confirmSafeStorage: I confirm I saved the PDF in a safe place I control.
backup.confirmTypeCode: Type the confirmation code from the PDF or from above
backup.loadVaultFailed: Could not load vault data for backup.

pdf.title: BitMacro Signer — offline vault backup
pdf.subtitle: Keep this file private. The passphrase you chose for the Signer vault decrypts the encrypted payload below (same algorithm as the hosted vault: PBKDF2 + AES-GCM).
pdf.criticalBullets: This file does not replace your passphrase — without it, recovery is impossible.|The nsec is never stored in plain text here; only ciphertext (blob) is included.|If someone obtains this file and your passphrase, they own your keys.|BitMacro cannot recover your passphrase or nsec.
pdf.passphraseReminder: Decrypt the vault fields below with the same passphrase you use to unlock BitMacro Signer.
pdf.codeTitle: Confirmation code (type this in Signer to continue)
pdf.codeBody: Signer shows this code to ensure you opened or saved this document. It is not a cryptographic secret.
pdf.qrTitle: QR — encrypted vault export (scan or transcribe JSON below if QR fails)
pdf.jsonTitle: Encrypted vault JSON (same data as QR)
pdf.recoverySteps: Use any tool that implements the same decryption (PBKDF2-SHA256, 600000 iterations, AES-GCM-256) with your passphrase on blob/salt/iv.|Decode your nsec from the decrypted material (bech32 nsec1…).|Import that nsec into any Nostr client.
pdf.footer: Format: bitmacro-signer-offline-vault v1. Open source: github.com/bitmacro/bitmacro-signer

sessions.title: Active sessions
sessions.body: NIP-46 does not send the app name: use the session key (npub/hex below). If you set a label when generating the QR, it is shown prominently.
sessions.noLabel: No label — set when generating the QR in onboarding
sessions.removeConfirm: Remove this client session? You will need a new QR or bunker link to connect again.
